meaning: n.建议者,支持者,[律]提出认证遗嘱者
examples: Halsey was identified as a leading proponent of the values of progressive education.
The most influential proponent of the coastal migration route has been Canadian archaeologist Knut Fladmark.
In 1938, at the government-convened National Health Conference, organized labor emerged as a major proponent of legislation to guarantee universal health care in the United States.
This caution is noteworthy because Church is not just a proponent of synthetic biology.
Now Amazon is a proponent of the MFA to remove any advantage from its competitors.
